title: anthemis cretica subsp. carpatica
intro: Anthemis cretica subsp. carpatica, also known as mountain chamomile, is a subspecies of Anthemis cretica, a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Eastern Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, soothing digestive issues, and reducing anxiety. The health benefits of mountain chamomile include calming effects, improved sleep quality, and relief from menstrual cramps, among others. The herbal preparations of Anthemis cretica subsp. carpatica can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to make topical creams and salves for skin care and wound healing.
